title: Oreocarya johnstonii
intro: Oreocarya johnstonii, also known as Johnston's Oreocarya, is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Oreocarya johnstonii include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Oreocarya johnstonii is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.

Within the genus, Oreocarya johnstonii is often distinguished from other species by its unique characteristics and geographical distribution. As a member of the Boraginaceae family, Oreocarya johnstonii shares similarities with other plants in this group, such as the ability to thrive in dry environments. The classification of Oreocarya johnstonii continues to be refined as new research and discoveries are made.
